subject: Support python3 in all plugins
commit_message:
Support python3 in all plugins

Unify tox/requirements/setup.py requirement specifications.
Do not set upper version limits if possible.
Drop uuid as dependency included with standard library.
Drop import of unmaintained cloudify_importer without python3 version.
Use PEP 508 URLs in requirements for non-PyPI (github) releases.
Use cloudify-common 5 release; pre-release package for python3.
Rewrite uses of map with loops/comprehensions.

Signed-off-by: Miroslav Los <miroslav.los@pantheon.tech>
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-1956
Change-Id: I7b3ceb97a628e3af5bda3178d182f4207069e86d

Patch Set 3:

OK, fair enough.

I consider == in a setup not great; it's for abstract reqs, > < are fine, but == is more of a requirements thing.

I'd like to see Mike Hwang's opinion here; can you please add him? He's the author of several plugins here too.

Patch Set 3:

Adding Mike. Mike, please see comments so far and share your thoughts to Miroslav.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> (4 comments)

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Adding Mike. Mike, please see comments so far and share your thoughts to Miroslav.

I'm catching up here..  Seems only k8s plugins install_requires got removed and not the others.  Is it because of the use of "==" in k8s plugin?  Shouldn't the install_requires keep the dependencies but in more abstract fashion?  My understanding is that install_requires defines the dependencies of the project.  Dependencies should be in both places in different forms is my thought: https://packaging.python.org/discussions/install-requires-vs-requirements/

In addition, what is the thought behind "Do not set upper version limits if possible.".  We use the upper bounds to prevent installing with versions of dependencies that are API breaking to avoid runtime errors before the project gets updated.

Patch Set 3:

it was commented out in multiple plugins

Patch Set 3:

sorry, I see that patch 2 and 3 changed things; my original comments were against patch 1 and are now outdated.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

my questions were addressed in patch 2/3

note that 37 may not work but that isn't an issue in this repo, it's due to the jjb for this repo; i support 37 being added here (38 is out now, too)

Patch Set 3:

Note that the k8s plugin has a problem as a result of this change, see JIRA DCAEGEN2-1985.
Also none of the plugin versions were incremented, so there are two different versions of each plugin with the same version number.
(I have only tested the k8s plugin.  Not sure about the rest.   Also not sure why we updated the CDAP and docker plugins, as they have not been used for several releases.  They should be deleted from the repo.)
